fighting_styles.name: Archery
fighting_styles.description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with Ranged weapons.

fighting_styles.name: Blind Fighting
fighting_styles.description: You have Blindsight with a range of 10 feet.

fighting_styles.name: Defense
fighting_styles.description: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

fighting_styles.name: Dueling
fighting_styles.description: When you are wielding a Melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

fighting_styles.name: Great Weapon Fighting
fighting_styles.description: When you roll damage for an attack you make with a Melee weapon that you are holding with two hands, you can treat any 1 or 2 on a damage die as a 3. The weapon must have the Two-Handed or Versatile property to gain this benefit.

fighting_styles.name: Interception
fighting_styles.description: When a creature you can see hits a target, other than you, within 5 feet of you with an attack, you can take a Reaction to reduce the damage the target takes by 1d10 plus your Proficiency Bonus (to a minimum of 0 damage). You must be wielding a Shield or a Simple or Martial weapon to use this Reaction.

fighting_styles.name: Protection
fighting_styles.description: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to interpose your Shield to impose Disadvantage on the attack roll. You must be wielding a Shield.

fighting_styles.name: Thrown Weapon Fighting
fighting_styles.description: When you hit with a ranged attack roll using a weapon that has the Thrown property, you gain a +2 bonus to the damage roll.

fighting_styles.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
fighting_styles.description: When you make an extra attack as a result of using a weapon that has the Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack if you aren't already adding it to the damage.

fighting_styles.name: Unarmed Fighting
fighting_styles.description:
When you hit with your Unarmed Strike and deal damage, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 plus your Strength modifier instead of the normal damage of an Unarmed Strike. If you aren't holding any weapons or a Shield when you make the attack roll, the d6 becomes a d8.

At the start of each of your turns, you can deal 1d4 Bludgeoning damage to one creature Grappled by you.
